reasoning: The FRED statistical trend analysis yields a combined probability of 0.55 for the VIX to increase past 14.91 within the next week (by 2025-12-28), factoring in both recent downward momentum and strong historical end-of-December positive seasonality. I use this 0.55 output as my primary anchor for the 1-week forecast. Over longer term horizons (1-month to January 20th and 3-month to March 21st), macroeconomic visibility decreases, meaning the series exhibits more of a random walk relative to the current forecast period. To explicitly follow the guideline that uncertainty must increase with forecast horizon (pulling distant probabilities closer to 0.5), I step the probabilities down smoothly to 0.53 for 1-month and 0.51 for 3-months.
